steps.title: Find the sentence boundary
steps.content: The quoted phrase completes the main clause about Waid's observation. The phrase an apt assessment is a supplementary noun phrase commenting on that observation.

steps.title: Reject faulty connectors
steps.content:
• Choice A: A semicolon plus and cannot join a main clause to a noun phrase.
  • Choice B: And makes it sound as if the novel depicts the upper classes as an apt assessment.
  • Choice D: No punctuation fails to mark the boundary.

steps.title: Use the supplementary comma
steps.content: A comma after the closing quotation mark correctly separates the completed observation from the explanatory noun phrase that follows. The answer is C.
